sentences.sentence: It is easy to imagine that the apparent chaos of casual conversation is a degenerate form of elegantly formulated prose—yet the natural state of language is interactive conversation, not monologue.

structure.subject: It
structure.predicate: is easy to imagine that the apparent chaos of casual conversation is a degenerate form of elegantly formulated prose—yet the natural state of language is interactive conversation, not monologue

sentences.sentence: But how can such chaotic interactions ever be meaningful?
Mere complex interaction surely can’t be enough in itself.
Otherwise, we would have to attribute meaning to the planets in our solar system, carrying out their intricately coordinated dance in elliptical circles around the sun.

structure.subject: we
structure.predicate: would have to attribute meaning to the planets in our solar system
structure.object: meaning to the planets in our solar system

semantic_roles.text_piece: the planets in our solar system

sentences.sentence: Again, we shall seek the answer in the game of charades.
